item.title: What did Men think the Wizards were?
item.description:
This is a short account on what kind of beings that humans thought that the undying Wizards were.
The War of the Ring occurred in year 3019 of the Third Age but already about year 1000 were signs of Sauron's return first felt in Middle-earth. Then the godlike Valar sent as emessaries five Istari, who Men called Wizards. Their task was to inspire peoples against the growing peril (art by Jacek Kopalski).
The Istari were spirits of the same kind as Sauron, but they were not as mighty as him. They "were forbidden to match his power with power, or to seek to dominate Elves or Men by force and fear" (RotK; Appendix A). And they must incarnate themselves "in flesh so as to treat on equality and win the trust of Elves and Men" (Unfinished Tales;IV;II, art by kittyninjafish).
"They came therefore in the shape of Men. though they were never young and aged only slowly, and they had many powers of mind and hand" (RotK; Appendix A, art by Norloth). A handful of Elves knew their true origin, and others guessed that they were servants of the Valar. But except for among the Dúnedain few Men were properly familiar with the Valar or the spirits who served them. So what did humans - the Dúnedain seemingly included - think of the Wizards?
"They first appeared in Middle-earth about the year 1000 of the Third Age, but for long they went about in simple guise, as it were of Men already old in years but hale in body, travellers and wanderers, gaining knowledge of Middle-earth and all that dwelt therein, but revealing to none their powers and purposes." – Unfinished Tales;IV;II, onwards (art by Gabriele Dell'otto)
"In that time Men saw them seldom and heeded them little. But as the shadow of Sauron began to grow and take shape again, they became more active and sought ever to contest the growth of the Shadow, and to move Elves and Men to beware of their peril." In the West this presumably means that the Istari became more active at some time between about 2060 and 2460 (art by Ralph Damiani).
Dates may differ in the East but the outcome ought to been the same (art by TurnerMohan): "Then far and wide rumour of their comings and goings, and their meddling in many matters, was noised about the Men; and Men perceived that they did not die, but remained the same (unless it were that they aged somewhat in looks), while the fathers and sons of Men passed away."
"Men, therefore, grew to fear them, even when they loved them, and they were held to be of the Elven-race (with whom, indeed, they often consorted)." This despite their aged features (though as seen with Círdan of the Havens also Elves could appear old and have beards). The view of the Wizards as Elvish is seen in the name northern Men gave to the Grey Wizard: Gandalf, meaning "Elf-of-the-wand" or "Wand-elf" (art by John Howe).
Some related accounts (art by Ted Nasmith): The Istari https://imgur.com/gallery/CnSexTO Blue Wizards [extract] https://imgur.com/gallery/7z39EBH Saruman https://imgur.com/gallery/T09mRkL Radagast https://imgur.com/gallery/ZwJe2J4 Elf beards https://imgur.com/gallery/qBrS1cm – – – – My Tolkien lore posts index: https://imgur.com/gallery/axV2mPF My Youtube-channel: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCL-RQ7sj9r1ZQw9Tl8cpPJA
item.dc:creator: Ngugi

item.title: Therapeutic posting.
item.description: My juvenile medical records packet was 257 pages (a little more than 1/2 of a standard 500 page ream of paper). I made it 7 pages in and started bawling. I was born ~ 12 weeks premature, to a mother tha made no effort to maintain a lifestyle healthy for prenatal care. I spent the first 72 hours in a heated incubator in a medically induced coma due to an inability to regulate gestational diabetes and what now would likely be described as acute multiple-narcotic dependency. I spent much of the first year quite ill. I was diagnosed as ADHD/ other. I didn’t speak in full sentences until close to my fifth birthday, due to an intense stutter (I spent years undertaking speech therapy, all to have it come out as an adult due to chronic fatigue syndrome). My birth mother had Bi-polar disorder, narcotic dependencies, and what would today be described as Borderline Personality Disorder. As a result I was doped like race horse, with no real ability to self-advocate. Through a childhood marked with abuse, neglect, and events so traumatic, a lot of my childhood is a blur with few shining moments. I was a little bit of a hellion in my youth, with a short stay in juvenile detention (nothing major) - but my time in front of the judge was marred by my birth mother standing up and advising that I was a juvenile delinquent and it would serve the interests of all involved if I was remanded to the state until my 18th birthday. Juvenile medical records showed that I’d seen more chemical cocktails than “reasonable”. And that there was concern the mixture would result in psychological and motor skills impact. Adolescence wasn’t easy, and I was a fighter. When I was 16, my father dropped me in front of the intake office at the Department of Corrections. The intake officer wasn’t on duty; they weren’t accepting wards of the state. We didn’t speak for weeks after that. Through a series of MRI’s, medical records showed what appeared to be a series of healed head injuries and Traumatic Brain Injuries. When I was 18 I dropped off the face of the earth and stopped taking calls from either parents. I’ve since gained a semblance of a relationship with my father but no relationship with my birth mother. I try to be the advocate I wish I could’ve had in my youth, and I try to be the kind of honorable man I wish there were more of in the world. TL;DR I requested juvenile medical records to try to get an understanding of why I have the issues I have. I’m so beyond tired, I don’t have the stomach to be mad. Bad joke to wrap it up: Skeleton walks into a Bar. Bartender asks what’ll it be. Skeleton says a beer and a mop.
item.dc:creator: dumblastname

item.title: Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Puckett Week)
item.description:
Welcome to the 191st edition in this series, let's do some good today. Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Days 181-190) https://imgur.com/a/g3sTR0J Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Days 171-180) https://imgur.com/a/7OnodoA Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Days 161-170) https://imgur.com/a/3SfMNfg Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Days 151-160) https://imgur.com/a/a3npRvx Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (Days 100-150) https://imgur.com/a/rdkioD9 Here are 20 wholesome things, you deserve them (First 100 days) https://imgur.com/a/SGVY2vD
I leave little notes on my coworker's desk sometimes They're all optimistic and Wu Tang themed
PJ turned 2 yesterday! Nanna turned 16 Monday!!! I made a post each day if you missed it!
This amazing image @carlsaganforlife made me for Puckett Week. Friday is the 2 year anniversary of Mr Puckett the dog passing away. In his honor, I started Puckett Week to help survivors of domestic violence facing homelessness. The Mary Parrish Center offers housing to survivors along with every piece of support they need to leave an abuser, stay safe and start their life fresh again. This week we can help.
If you'd like to help domestic violence survivors facing homelessness there are two ways in Mr Puckett's memory Donations, no matter how small https://www.maryparrish.org/donate I also had them set up an Amazon Wishlist if you'd like to send items directly to survivors https://www.amazon.com/hz/wishlist/ls/3USHUHIVSINQE?ref_=wl_share ... Thank you for reading my post, I'll see everyone again tomorrow
item.dc:creator: MrPuckett

item.title: It's a dump of collected things. None of it was created by me.
item.description:
Ha! A pun!
He'll burn the universe down.
It's typical because of the Olympics.
Such a great movie.
Tonight, the lone wolf rides in a pack of one.
You couldn't throw a fork or something?
Apparently, "granny" is not a professional style
So true to life....
Take my wife! Please!
He's only mostly dead.
Still haven't made this but it's been on my phone for awhile.
Hamsters are cute.
3.14
What a fun bag this would be to play with.
When asking Jr. High kids who did that...
RIP
TV, the Wordle of the 1980s.
Circles
Heh! Butt!
Satire.
How Jr. High kids respond to directions.
Telling it like it is
Deep thoughts
Calvin and Hobbes is the greatest
Wizard
Shephard
Captain
That's pretty cool
Scotch!
One of my favorite parodies from the master, Weird Al
Everything should be explained via D&D
Snoop
Smug!

item.title: Dump with historical photos from the practice of medicine
item.description:
1. Wooden prosthetic hand c. 1800
2. US Civil War surgeon's kit
3. From Walter Reed Physiotherapy Store, 1920's
4. Antique birthing chair used until the 1800's
5. Radioactive water
6. Lewis Sayre's scoliosis treatment
7. Obstetric Phantom 1700-1800. Device to teach medical students and midwives about childbirth
8. Antique prosthetic leg
9. Dr.Clark's spinal apparatus advertisement approx. 1878
10. Device measuring metabolism
11. Syringes
12. Cough Syrup consisting of alcohol, cannabis and chloroform
13. Dental x-ray
14. "Random" Nervous pills for women
15. DIY nose shaper before plastic surgery
16. At home x-ray machines for medical practitioners giving x-rays in the safety of their homes
17. The Ritter Orthodontist work tower
18. A midwifes bag
19. Radiology nurse technician WWI 1918
item.dc:creator: Norwegianwoods

item.title: Day 264: of cheeseburgers I want inside of me
item.description: Spicycado Double Beef Patty, Emmentaler Cheese, Jalapenos, Chillimajo, Red Onion, Avocado, Lettuce From Burger Mansion In Stockholm & Göteborg, Sweden Previous Day 263: https://imgur.com/gallery/8gr5Wou MESSAGE ME BURGER PICS OR YOUR FAVORITE BURGER SPOTS! Map of all burger spots that are available on yelp! Big thanks to @OhBoyDoIHaveNews4U for this. Link to his post and map https://imgur.com/gallery/vcObDuD
